summary: The shared `SITE_URL` foundation is implemented correctly in code: `src/features/public-site/config.ts` is the single origin parser/builder, `src/features/public-site/metadata.ts` centralizes canonical/robots behavior, and `/` plus `/notes/[slug]` consume that helper instead of guessing from request context. Required checks also passed per the deterministic summary. The blocking issue is the operator/runtime contract: `docker-compose.yml` does not pass `SITE_URL` into the container, while `.env.compose.example` and the docs now present `SITE_URL` as part of the supported Compose configuration. That leaves the Docker/self-host path misaligned with the documented source-of-truth boundary, so task 087 is not complete in substance yet.
satisfied_exit_criteria: There is one implemented source of truth for canonical origin handling in `src/features/public-site/config.ts`.
Homepage and note-page metadata now rely on the shared helper in `src/app/page.tsx` and `src/app/notes/[slug]/page.tsx`.
Missing or invalid `SITE_URL` fails closed at the metadata/helper layer, with unit coverage in `tests/unit/public-site-metadata.test.ts`.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, and `npm run verify` passed in the provided deterministic check summary.
missing_requirements: The shipped Compose runtime does not forward `SITE_URL` to the app (`docker-compose.yml` environment block), so Docker deployments cannot actually use the documented canonical-origin contract.
Operator docs are still incomplete/misaligned for the Compose path: `README.md` describes `.env` as auth/port/demo/AI config only and omits `SITE_URL`, despite the new public-origin requirement.
human_review_triggers: Compose/operator contract drift: the repo documents `SITE_URL` for Docker usage, but the shipped `docker-compose.yml` does not provide it to the container.
